seo.title: Building Agentic RAG with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build agentic rag with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.
keywords: Azure AI Agent Service agentic rag

content.intro: Azure AI Agent Service enables building sophisticated Agentic RAG systems that autonomously plan queries, retrieve from multiple sources like Bing Search and Azure AI Search, and synthesize responses with attribution using Azure OpenAI models. Guardrails are essential to mitigate risks like policy-violating tool outputs, outdated grounding data, and token limit breaches that undermine enterprise reliability. Vex integrates as middleware to verify source faithfulness, detect hallucinations, and ensure cross-source consistency in high-risk deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: Agentic RAG Architecture with Azure AI Agent Service
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture features an Azure AI Agent that receives user queries and uses Azure OpenAI for planning multi-step retrieval tasks. It invokes tools like Bing Search for web grounding and Azure AI Search for enterprise file retrieval, then synthesizes results with citation tracking. Agents maintain thread state across iterations, handling complex reasoning while respecting enterprise security via Azure RBAC.

Key components include the Agent Thread for conversation history, Tool Calls for retrieval actions, and Response Synthesis that combines retrieved chunks with LLM generation. Azure-specific metadata from file search is filtered, and Bing results are grounded against freshness policies. Token limits are monitored per thread to prevent overflow.

Vex middleware intercepts agent outputs post-tool execution and pre-final response, validating against enterprise policies and verification needs like hallucination detection and citation accuracy.

risks.name: Tool execution results not validated against enterprise policies
risks.description: Agents execute Bing Search or file retrieval without checking outputs against DLP policies or sensitivity labels
risks.mitigation: Vex middleware scans tool results for PII, compliance violations before synthesis

risks.name: Grounding data from Bing Search containing outdated information
risks.description: Real-time web results may include stale or incorrect data not verified for recency
risks.mitigation: Vex validates result dates and cross-references with knowledge cutoff

risks.name: File search returning Azure-specific metadata instead of content
risks.description: Azure AI Search returns index metadata (doc_id, chunk_id) instead of actual content
risks.mitigation: Vex extracts and validates content fields from search responses

risks.name: Agent thread token limits exceeded without graceful degradation
risks.description: Long conversations exceed context windows without summarization or truncation
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors token usage and triggers summarization before limits hit

implementation.title: Create Azure AI Agent with RAG Tools
implementation.description: Initialize agent with Bing Search and Azure AI Search tools for multi-source retrieval
implementation.code:
from azure.ai.projects import Agent, BingSearchTool, AzureAISearchTool

agent = Agent(
    name="agentic-rag-agent",
    instructions="Plan queries across Bing and enterprise search, cite sources",
    model="gpt-4o",
    tools=[
        BingSearchTool(max_results=5),
        AzureAISearchTool(
            endpoint="https://your-search-service.search.windows.net",
            index_name="enterprise-rag-index",
            api_key="your-api-key"
        )
    ]
)
thread = agent.create_thread()
response = agent.run(thread_id=thread.id, message="Find latest Q3 earnings data")

implementation.title: TypeScript Agent with Retrieval Tools
implementation.description: Configure agent with search tools using Azure AI Projects SDK

implementation.title: Multi-Step Query Planning
implementation.description: Agent autonomously decomposes complex queries across sources
implementation.code:
from azure.ai.projects import Agent

agent = Agent(
    name="multi-step-rag",
    instructions="""1. Identify key entities
2. Search Bing for recent news
3. Search enterprise docs for internal data
4. Synthesize with citations""",
    model="gpt-4o"
)

response = agent.run(
    thread_id=thread.id,
    message="Compare our Q3 revenue vs industry benchmarks"
)
print(response.content[0].text)

vexIntegration.title: Final Response Verification with Vex
vexIntegration.description: Validate synthesized response for hallucination and citation accuracy
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexClient

vex = VexClient(api_key="vex-api-key")

# After agent.run()
final_verdict = vex.verify({
    "type": "agentic_rag",
    "response": response.content[0].text,
    "citations": extract_citations(response),
    "verifications": [
        "source_faithfulness",
        "hallucination_detection", 
        "cross_source_consistency",
        "citation_verification"
    ],
    "enterprise_policy": "block_pii"
})

if final_verdict.passed:
    print(final_verdict.response)
else:
    print(f"[VEX BLOCKED] Issues: {final_verdict.issues}")
    # Trigger human review or fallback response
